# Definitions
* An automata, denoted $\alpha$, is an infinite series of frames, denoted $\alpha_{k}$ where $k \in \mathbb{Z} \land k \geq 0$, generated by a function $\alpha_{\Omega}$ operating on the initial input frame $\alpha_{0}$. $\alpha_{k}=\alpha_{\Omega}(\alpha_{k-1})$.}
* A frame, $\alpha_{k}$ has two components: its time-step, given by $k$, and the state it encodes, given by its value.
